message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save(). I need to edit src/PIL/Image.py (save method) and Tests/test_image.py. Please let me know your feature and which files you need so we can keep edits disjoint - if you also need Image.py we should coordinate carefully on non-overlapping sections.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=/backup_suffix= params to Image.save() in Image.py; touching Image.py's save() method only. Please tell me which files/areas you'll own so we can avoid overlap, especially if you also need to touch Image.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed, we both need to touch Image.py's save() and test_image.py - unavoidable overlap since we're both adding kwargs to save(). I'll add my dry_run param to the save() signature and implement it in the body; you add backup/backup_suffix. We'll each work independently and resolve the merge conflict in save()'s signature/body afterward by combining both sets of changes. I'll keep my changes minimal and localized (mainly in Image.save around the final write step) to make merging easy.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need Image.py's save() and its tests - can't split by whole file, so let's split by section instead. My feature: backup=False, backup_suffix='.bak' kwargs added at the END of save()'s existing kwarg list; my backup-copy logic goes as the FIRST statements inside save() body (right after docstring, before any existing code), using shutil.copy2 gated on os.path.exists(fp). My tests: new test methods named test_save_backup_* appended to Tests/test_image.py, not touching existing tests. Please place your dry_run kwarg also at the end of the signature (after mine) and keep your logic further down in the body (e.g. near the actual write/open-fp step) so our diffs land on different lines. I'll merge your branch into mine via git merge afterward and resolve any conflicts by hand. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Confirmed - proceeding to implement backup/backup_suffix now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Sounds good. Exact plan: I'll add 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' near the existing 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)' line (~line 2546, well after the docstring/your backup code at the top of the body). My actual logic change is in the open_fp/write block near the end of save() (~lines 2561-2590): when dry_run is True, fp becomes an io.BytesIO() instead of opening/writing the real file, and I skip closing/removing the real file in that case. I won't touch the top of the body where your backup-copy logic lives. Tests: I'll add new test methods (test_save_dry_run_*) appended to Tests/test_image.py, not touching existing tests. This should merge cleanly.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implemented backup=/backup_suffix= kwargs. Changes: (1) added 'import shutil' at top, (2) popped backup/backup_suffix from params right after docstring in save(), (3) added shutil.copy2 backup logic inside 'if open_fp:' block near 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' (before the params.get('append') check) - this is BEFORE the final write step so should not collide with your dry_run logic near the actual write. Added 4 new test methods (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_no_backup) right before test_pathlib in Tests/test_image.py. All tests pass. Let me know when you're done so I can merge.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implemented dry_run: params.pop near save_all pop (~line 2547), and open_fp/write block now uses io.BytesIO() when dry_run=True instead of opening/writing the real file (skips close/remove of real file too). Added docstring note + 3 tests (test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_fp, test_save_dry_run_raises). Full test_image.py suite passes (195 passed). Committing and pushing now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Merged your team/agent2 branch into agent1. Resolved the test_image.py conflict by keeping both sets of new tests (my 3 dry_run tests + your 4 backup tests). Image.py merged cleanly with no conflict markers - your backup shutil.copy2 call sits before my dry_run branch's io.BytesIO() swap, and since dry_run implies no real file is touched, backups are correctly skipped too. Full test_image.py suite passes: 199 passed. Pushed merged branch to team/agent1 and wrote patch.txt.
